title: Choosing a Gift for Mum
summary: A brother and sister discuss ideas for their mother's birthday present.

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: Hey, Mum's birthday is on Sunday. We need to buy her a present.

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: I know! I was thinking about that too. Do you have any ideas?

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: What about flowers? Mum loves flowers.

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: Flowers are nice, but they don't last very long. I want to give her something she can keep.

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: Good point. What about a book? She likes reading.

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: That's a good idea! Do you know what kind of books she likes?

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: She likes stories about travel and cooking. She has a lot of cookbooks already, though.

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: Then maybe a travel book? Or we could buy her a nice scarf. She always wears scarves in winter.

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: Oh, I like that idea! A scarf is useful and she can wear it every day.

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: We could also get her a card and write something special inside.

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: Yes! That is a lovely idea. She always keeps our cards. How much should we spend?

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: I have twenty dollars. If you add twenty dollars too, we can spend forty dollars in total.

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: That sounds good. We can find a beautiful scarf for forty dollars.

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: Let's go to the shops on Saturday afternoon. Is that okay for you?

body.speaker: Brother
body.text: Saturday is perfect. I can't wait to see Mum's face when she opens it!

body.speaker: Sister
body.text: Me too! She is going to love it.

annotations.exampleEn: Good point! I didn't think of that.
Fresh flowers don't last very long in hot weather.
She has many cookbooks on the kitchen shelf.
He wore a red scarf around his neck in the cold weather.
A good dictionary is very useful for language learners.

annotations.meaningVi: viết điều gì đó đặc biệt
annotations.exampleEn: She wrote something special inside the birthday card.
How much do you want to spend on the gift?
We spent fifty dollars in total on her present.

annotations.meaningVi: tôi rất háo hức / tôi nóng lòng chờ
annotations.exampleEn: I can't wait to see you next weekend!

annotations.phrase: she is going to love it
annotations.meaningVi: cô ấy sẽ rất thích nó
annotations.exampleEn: This is the perfect gift — she is going to love it!
She always wears a hat and scarf in winter.
What a lovely idea to write a poem for her!

grammarNotes.title: Suggestions with 'What about' and 'could'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'What about + noun/verb-ing' để đề xuất: 'What about flowers?' / 'What about a book?' Dùng 'could' để đề nghị: 'We could buy her a nice scarf.' / 'We could also get her a card.' Đây là những cách đề xuất lịch sự và phổ biến trong hội thoại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'What about + noun/gerund' to make suggestions: 'What about flowers?' / 'What about a book?' Use 'could' for suggestions: 'We could buy her a nice scarf.' / 'We could also get her a card.' These are polite, common ways to suggest in conversation.

grammarNotes.title: Future with 'going to'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Going to + verb' diễn đạt kế hoạch trong tương lai hoặc điều chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra: 'She is going to love it.' / 'Let's go to the shops on Saturday.' Dùng 'going to' khi bạn đã có kế hoạch hoặc dự đoán dựa trên bằng chứng hiện tại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Going to + verb' expresses future plans or certainty: 'She is going to love it.' / 'Let's go to the shops on Saturday.' Use 'going to' when you have a plan or make a prediction based on present evidence.

translationVi: Anh trai: Này, sinh nhật Mẹ là Chủ Nhật. Chúng ta cần mua quà cho Mẹ. Em gái: Tôi biết! Tôi cũng đang nghĩ về điều đó. Anh có ý tưởng gì không? Anh trai: Tặng hoa thì sao? Mẹ rất thích hoa. Em gái: Hoa thì đẹp, nhưng không tươi lâu. Tôi muốn tặng Mẹ thứ gì đó có thể giữ được lâu. Anh trai: Ý hay đấy. Tặng sách thì sao? Mẹ thích đọc sách. Em gái: Ý tưởng hay! Anh có biết Mẹ thích loại sách gì không? Anh trai: Mẹ thích truyện về du lịch và nấu ăn. Nhưng Mẹ đã có nhiều sách dạy nấu ăn rồi. Em gái: Vậy có thể tặng sách du lịch? Hoặc chúng ta có thể mua cho Mẹ một chiếc khăn quàng cổ đẹp. Mẹ hay đeo khăn quàng cổ vào mùa đông. Anh trai: Ồ, tôi thích ý tưởng đó! Khăn quàng cổ rất hữu ích và Mẹ có thể đeo hàng ngày. Em gái: Chúng ta cũng có thể mua thiệp và viết điều gì đó đặc biệt bên trong. Anh trai: Có! Đó là ý tưởng tuyệt vời. Mẹ luôn giữ thiệp của chúng ta. Chúng ta nên chi bao nhiêu? Em gái: Tôi có hai mươi đô. Nếu anh thêm hai mươi đô nữa, chúng ta có thể chi tổng cộng bốn mươi đô. Anh trai: Nghe hay đó. Chúng ta có thể tìm được một chiếc khăn đẹp với bốn mươi đô. Em gái: Chúng ta đi mua sắm vào chiều thứ Bảy nhé. Anh có rảnh không? Anh trai: Thứ Bảy hoàn hảo. Tôi nóng lòng muốn thấy mặt Mẹ khi mở quà! Em gái: Tôi cũng vậy! Mẹ sẽ rất thích.

questions.prompt: Why does the sister not want to buy flowers?
questions.explanation: 'Flowers are nice, but they don't last very long.'
questions.hint: Find the sister's reason for rejecting flowers.

questions.prompt: What kind of books does Mum like?
questions.explanation: 'She likes stories about travel and cooking.'
questions.hint: Find the brother's comment about Mum's reading.

questions.prompt: Why don't they choose a cookbook?
questions.explanation: 'She has a lot of cookbooks already.'
questions.hint: Find the reason they do not buy a cookbook.

questions.prompt: What gift do they decide to buy?
Why is a scarf a good gift for Mum?
options: She always wears scarves in winter
questions.hint: Find the sister's reason for suggesting a scarf.

questions.prompt: What else do they decide to get along with the scarf?
questions.explanation: 'We could also get her a card and write something special inside.'

questions.prompt: How much money does the sister have?
How much will they spend in total?
questions.explanation: 'we can spend forty dollars in total'

questions.prompt: When do they plan to go shopping?
questions.explanation: 'Let's go to the shops on Saturday afternoon.'

questions.prompt: What is the brother excited to see?
options: Mum's face when she opens the gift
questions.explanation: 'I can't wait to see Mum's face when she opens it!'

questions.prompt: What does the sister say at the end?
options: She thinks Mum will be surprised
She says Mum is going to love it
She wants to buy another gift too
questions.hint: Read the last line of the dialogue.

cloze.template: Brother: Mum's birthday is on {{b1}}. We need to buy her a present. Sister: Do you have any ideas? Brother: What about {{b2}}? Mum loves them. Sister: They are nice, but they don't {{b3}} very long. I want to give her something she can keep. Brother: What about a book? She likes {{b4}}. Sister: Do you know what kind of books she likes? Brother: She likes stories about travel and {{b5}}. She has a lot of cookbooks already. Sister: Then maybe a {{b6}}? She always wears scarves in winter. Brother: A scarf is {{b7}} and she can wear it every day. Sister: We could also get her a {{b8}} and write something special inside. Brother: She always keeps our cards. How much should we {{b9}}? Sister: I have twenty {{b10}}. If you add twenty dollars too, we can spend forty dollars in total. Brother: We can find a beautiful scarf for forty dollars. Sister: Let's go to the shops on Saturday {{b11}}. Brother: I can't wait to see Mum's face when she {{b12}} it! Sister: Me too! She is going to {{b13}} it.

blanks.explanation: 'We could buy her a nice scarf.'
'A scarf is useful and she can wear it every day.'
'we could also get her a card'
'Let's go to the shops on Saturday afternoon.'
'I can't wait to see Mum's face when she opens it!'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The brother and sister work together to choose a gift for their mother. Do you think it is better to give a practical gift or a special gift? What would you choose as the perfect gift for someone you love?
